name: Pico Mentiras y Peña Palomera - trail running in the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
name_original: Pico Mentiras y Peña Palomera - trailrunning por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
region_intro: Jogging around Segura De La Sierra offers diverse landscapes within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. The region features a wide mountain range with significant forest wealth, providing varied elevations for running. Extensive pine forests offer shaded routes, while rivers like the Guadalquivir and Segura originate here, creating picturesque valleys and gorges. The terrain includes plateaus, cliffs, and peaks, with elevations reaching up to 1,993m. Best jogging routes around Segura De La Sierra The most popular jogging route is Cordillera Subbética – trail running through the Sierra and Summit of La Sagra, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is El Yelmo - trail running in the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park, a difficult 11.8 miles (18.9 km) path. This route explores the natural park, offering views towards El Yelmo peak. Local runners also love the Cerro de la Buitrera - circular trail running from la Puerta de Segura, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through varied mountain landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. Jogging around Segura De La Sierra is defined by mountainous terrain, extensive forested zones, and river valleys. The network offers options primarily for experienced runners, with 22 out of 24 routes rated as difficult. The routes in Segura De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. More than 170 runners have used komoot to explore Segura De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Segura De La Sierra, and what is their general difficulty?
A: There are over 20 jogging routes in the Segura De La Sierra region. The terrain is predominantly mountainous, with 22 out of 24 routes rated as difficult, offering a significant challenge for experienced runners. There are also 2 moderate routes for those seeking a slightly less strenuous option.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The region is characterized by an 'orographic labyrinth' of diverse terrain. You'll encounter extensive pine forests, varied elevations from plateaus to peaks reaching nearly 2,000m, and dramatic features like cliffs and gorges. Routes often follow river valleys, offering a mix of challenging inclines and scenic paths.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are circular running routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989782042/cerro-de-la-buitrera-circular-trail-running-from-la-puerta-de-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro de la Buitrera - circular trail running from la Puerta de Segura&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 7.9-mile (12.7 km) route leading through varied mountain landscapes.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2064086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt;, pass through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despiernacaballos Pass&lt;/a&gt;, or run towards peaks like El Yelmo and La Sagra. The area also features stunning viewpoints, extensive forests, and dramatic rock formations where vultures breed.

Q: Are there any family-friendly or easy jogging routes in Segura De La Sierra?
A: While most routes are rated as difficult, there are 2 moderate routes available. The mountainous terrain and significant elevation changes mean that many trails require a good level of fitness. For families, it's advisable to check the specific route details for elevation and distance to ensure it's suitable for all participants.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The running routes in Segura De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. Over 170 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging mountain trails and the stunning natural beauty of the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for trail running in Segura De La Sierra?
A: The region offers diverse conditions throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for running through the extensive forests. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, but higher peaks like El Yelmo and Las Banderillas offer cooler conditions. Winters can bring snow to the highest points, adding a different challenge for experienced trail runners.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Segura De La Sierra?
A: Segura de la Sierra is located within the Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, a protected area. While many natural parks in Spain allow dogs, specific rules regarding leashes and restricted areas (especially near wildlife or protected habitats) may apply. It's recommended to check local park regulations or signage at trailheads for the most current information on dog-friendly policies.

Q: Where can I find parking for trailheads in Segura De La Sierra?
A: Specific parking information varies by trailhead. Many routes start from villages or accessible points within the Natural Park. For routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986576312/cordillera-subbetica-trail-running-through-the-sierra-and-summit-of-la-sagra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cordillera Subbética – trail running through the Sierra and Summit of La Sagra&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989188872/el-yelmo-trail-running-in-the-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas-sierras-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo - trail running in the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park&lt;/a&gt;, you'll typically find designated parking areas near the starting points, often in or near small towns or visitor centers. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for specific parking recommendations.

Q: Are there any shelters or huts along the running routes?
A: Yes, there are several shelters and huts in the wider Segura De La Sierra area that could be useful for longer runs or multi-day adventures. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3980308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo del Espino Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5524225" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Era del Fustal Shelter&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6002950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Cañada Humosa&lt;/a&gt;. These can provide a place to rest or take cover, especially in the more remote parts of the Natural Park.

Q: What is the longest running route available in Segura De La Sierra?
A: One of the longer and more challenging routes is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989188872/el-yelmo-trail-running-in-the-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas-sierras-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo - trail running in the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 11.8 miles (18.9 km) and features significant elevation changes as it explores the natural park towards El Yelmo peak.

Q: Are there opportunities for trail running events in the Segura De La Sierra region?
A: Yes, the area is a recognized hub for trail running, hosting events like 'Sierra Segura Infinita'. These events offer various distances through the Natural Park's trails and forest tracks, catering to mountain runners and indicating a strong community and infrastructure for the sport.
tips:
The iconic Peña Palomera is located in the Calar de la Sima, a typical example of a Betic calar. It is a formidable mountain range located between the provinces of Albacete and Jaén. Its relief extends from north to south for more than five kilometers in which it maintains a constant height of around 1800 meters, which gives it a special and superior rank over its neighbors that barely reach 1700 in specific places.

The most characteristic feature of this calar is an enormous molar located in the geographical center of the divide known as the Peña Palomera, while the maximum elevation is the Cerrico or peak of the Mentiras of 1896 meters and which is barely distinguishable in the enormity of the rope, which the locals call Vipers.

The Mentiras peak is located in the Calar de la Sima. It is the highest peak in the area and the second in Albacete.

On your way up you find La Sima, which gives its name to this Calar. From its summit it is worth stopping for a few minutes to contemplate the impressive panoramic views, which can be seen across the entire area.
